description: Actionable investigative threads. Relational data only. Analysis in analysis/leads.md

records.title: Wright Patterson AFB — Directed Energy / Particle Beam Programs 1943-1960
records.archive: NARA RG342 / Wright Patterson AFB FOIA office / NARA RG340 Air Force
records.note: UPGRADED: 1983 classified FBI memo confirms Tesla papers reportedly sent from OAPC to 'Equipment Lab' at WPAFB, now closed/reorganized. WPAFB Foreign Technology Division researcher Butler actively searching in 1983. AMTORG CONTRACT (Part 3): Tesla signed contract April 20 1935 with Amtorg, received $25,000 for teleforce specifications. This contract was in the papers reportedly transferred to WPAFB Equipment Lab.

records.title: Authorization chain — who selected Trump as evaluator
records.archive: NARA RG227 (OSRD/NDRC records), MIT Archives
records.note: PARTIAL: Trump's letter names Walter Gorsuch (APC NY) and Joseph T. King (APC Washington) as requestors. Trump signed as Technical Aide, Division 14 NDRC (radar division). Who above Gorsuch/King authorized ONI participation? That chain is unresolved.

records.title: Vannevar Bush personal papers — MIT Archives — Tesla references
records.archive: MIT Institute Archives MC 78
records.method: Physical archive visit or paid researcher

records.title: Kenneth [REDACTED] — identity confirmed
records.resolved_identity: Kenneth M. Swezey, 163 Milton Street, Brooklyn NY. Writer for Popular Mechanics. Personally present at safe opening Jan 8 1943. Wrote directly to Hoover June 1955 re missing Edison Medal. Hoover personally replied with denial template.
records.archive: FBI FOIA (Swezey file), Smithsonian Institution Archives
records.method: Confirmed in Part 1 FBI vault document

records.title: National Archives OAPC records — physical chain of custody for 20 missing trunks
records.archive: NARA Archives II College Park MD — RG131, RG227
records.method: NARA online request or physical visit
records.note: Highest priority physical archive lead. The paper trail for 20 trunks legally must exist.

records.title: Helen Gandy destroyed Hoover personal files 1972 — any Tesla material documented
records.archive: Congressional Record 1972-1974 hearings, GPO

records.title: Yugoslav diplomatic archives — Kosanović cables 1943-1951
records.archive: Nikola Tesla Museum Belgrade, Serbian National Archives
records.method: Direct contact with Tesla Museum Belgrade

records.title: Morgan Bank / Morgan Library — post-1904 Tesla correspondence
records.archive: Morgan Library & Museum NYC — Pierpont Morgan papers

records.title: Henry Wallace diaries — University of Iowa — January 1943 entries
records.archive: University of Iowa Special Collections MS 0290
records.method: Check online finding aid first, then request
records.note: Upgraded: Lozado (Wallace advisor) confirmed in FBI Part 1 as direct link on day of death. Wallace diary entry for Jan 7-8 1943 should show what he knew and when.

records.title: Willis George — ONI Third Naval District — authorization for Jan 26-27 examination
records.archive: NARA RG38 (Office of Naval Intelligence records), Third Naval District files
records.note: NEW: George plus two Navy yeomen participated in civilian APC examination. No explanation in file. ONI participation in a probate/APC examination is procedurally anomalous. Who authorized it?

records.title: WPAFB Equipment Lab — organizational successor — Tesla paper repository
records.archive: NARA RG340, Air Force Materiel Command history office, WPAFB
records.method: FOIA to WPAFB + AF history office contact
records.note: NEW: 1983 classified FBI memo names 'Equipment Lab, now closed or reorganized' at WPAFB as reported recipient of Tesla papers from OAPC. Reorganized labs transfer records. The successor organization holds what the Equipment Lab held.

records.title: Charlotte Muzar — estate agent for Kosanovich — 134 East 63rd St NY
records.archive: NY County Surrogate Court probate records, NARA RG131 OAPC
records.note: NEW: Muzar paid $15/month storage on Kosanovich's behalf. She was the US-side operational manager of the estate. Her correspondence with Kosanovich would document what instructions she received about inventory.

records.title: Dr. Lozado — Wallace advisor — identity and role on day of Tesla's death
records.archive: University of Iowa Wallace papers, National Archives VP correspondence
records.note: NEW: Lozado confirmed in FBI teletype as VP Wallace advisor who told Spanel government was 'vitally interested' on day of death. Identifying Lozado establishes whether notification was reactive or the VP's office was tracking Tesla already.

records.title: Tesla gold Edison Medal — missing from safe — chain of custody 1943-1951
records.archive: NARA RG131 OAPC shipping manifest, Tesla Museum Belgrade inventory, Port of Rijeka manifest
records.note: NEW: Swezey confirms medal was in safe when he left. Medal and keys missing on Belgrade arrival. Keys found in tin box OUTSIDE safe. Physical object with documented custody break during 1943-1951 storage/transit period.

records.title: Harvey F. Rath — FBI agent on Tesla case, resigned April 1946
records.archive: FBI personnel records (FOIA), DOJ archives, NJ business records ca 1946
records.note: NEW: Rath told Spanel FBI was 'stopped from a higher level' on Tesla case. Resigned April 5 1946. Never re-interviewed. Residence at resignation: Orange NJ. Closest known insider who broke cover about suppression. DATE DISCREPANCY: Nichols memo says resigned April 5 1946; Rath himself says May 1945. If 1945, Rath filed the Oct 17 1945 Bergstresser report AFTER he had already left — requires explanation.

records.title: Kosanovich-Silvermaster meeting December 11 1946 — Tesla papers still in US storage
records.archive: Silvermaster FBI file (partially released), Venona intercepts NARA/NSA, Bentley grand jury testimony
records.note: NEW: FBI documented Kosanovich met confirmed Soviet spy Nathan Gregory Silvermaster on Dec 11 1946. Tesla papers still in US storage at this date. No follow-up investigation documented in Part 2.

records.title: Louis Adamic papers — Princeton Mudd Manuscript Library
records.archive: Princeton University Mudd Manuscript Library, Adamic Papers
records.method: Contact Princeton library, finding aid online
records.note: NEW: Adamic was connecting node between Kosanovich, ASC, Yugoslav relief networks. Died August 4 1951 (ruled suicide), 5 months before Tesla effects shipped to Belgrade. His correspondence may contain Tesla/Fitzgerald/Kosanovich references.

records.title: Spanel Senate communications — furnished to DOJ September 19 1950
records.archive: DOJ records NARA, Congressional correspondence ca 1950
records.note: NEW: Spanel communicated to unnamed Senators that FBI was stopped 'from a higher level' on Tesla case. DOJ received these communications Sept 19 1950. Senators not named in FBI memo. Congressional Record may identify.

records.title: Amtorg contract original — Tesla/Amtorg signed agreement April 20 1935
records.archive: NARA RG131 (OAPC records), Trump evaluation original with exhibits
records.note: NEW: Trump abstract (Exhibit Q) documents signed Tesla-Amtorg contract for teleforce specifications, $25,000 fee, April 20 1935. Original signed document may be in OAPC/APC records. This is the primary documentary evidence for Soviet intelligence transaction.

records.title: Spanel financial relationship with Fitzgerald — Remington Arms and Higgins Ship Building records
records.archive: Remington Arms corporate records, Higgins Industries records, NARA military records ca 1942-1944
records.note: NEW: Spanel blocked Fitzgerald's Remington Arms deal, then controlled 60% of Fitzgerald's Higgins contract profits. Fitzgerald fired Nov 1943. Spanel was managing Tesla legacy access through Fitzgerald from Dec 1942.
